text:
You are working on beads issue str-hsrb: "Fallback boundary search for unknown/opaque constraints"

## Issue Details
When Z3 returns unknown or timeout for a constraint, and Shatter has inputs on both sides of a branch, use coverage-guided binary-search-style shrinking to find the boundary concretely. Currently falls back to naive fuzz_inputs() which is blind. Requires one input on each side (true-path witness, false-path witness). Interpolate/mutate between them, re-execute, track which side each result takes. More effective than random fuzzing because it has directional signal. Uses same shrink_candidates infrastructure.

Depends on str-u7sj (shrink candidates - done).
Parent epic: str-rc6i (Formal methods and property-based verification).

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="str-hsrb"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.

## Instructions
1. Follow CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md conventions
2. Read shrink_candidates infrastructure, explorer.rs, and orchestrator.rs to understand the current fallback behavior for unknown constraints
3. Implement boundary search: given true-path and false-path witnesses, interpolate between them to find the boundary
4. Integrate into the existing fuzz fallback path
5. Add unit tests and proptest coverage
6. Run quality gates: `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` in shatter-core
7. Run `/pre-completion` before reporting done — include its output in your completion message
8. Push your branch: `git push -u origin HEAD`

When done, mark task #10 as completed via TaskUpdate and send a message to the team lead.

When Z3 returns unknown/timeout, use coverage-guided binary-search shrinking between true/false witnesses to find the boundary. Uses shrink_candidates infrastructure. Depends on str-u7sj (done). Run /pre-completion before reporting done.","assignedBy":"str-hsrb","timestamp":"2026-03-08T03:55:54.368Z"}

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1772942669391-ynxmkh6","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
str-hsrb: implement boundary search fallback for unknown/opaque constraints

When Z3 can't solve a branch constraint (Unknown or timeout) and both
sides of the branch have been observed, interpolate between the true-path
and false-path witnesses using binary-search-style narrowing. More
effective than blind fuzz_inputs() because the search has directional
signal from observed branch outcomes.

- New boundary_search module with type-aware interpolation (Int, Float,
  Array, Object, Nullable, Union)
- Integrated into orchestrator loop as higher-priority fallback than
  blind fuzzing (InputSource::BoundarySearch between Fuzzed and Drilled)
- Added DiscoveryMethod::BoundarySearch for attribution tracking
- 27 tests: unit tests + proptest coverage for invariants

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"","description":"Commit boundary search implementation"},"permission_updates":[]}}
